title: Pair 07: FCFS vs Best-Fit Seating
summary: This comparison keeps the burger hall, a mixed dinner wave, and service pace fixed, but changes how open tables are assigned. Option A seats parties with the earliest suitable table, while Option B uses best-fit matching to test whether tighter table matching improves flow when pairs, fours, and larger groups overlap.

A.label: FCFS earliest suitable
A.notes: Hosts assign the earliest suitable open table during a busy dinner wave where mixed party sizes overlap.

B.label: Best-fit table matching
B.notes: Hosts bias toward the tightest fitting open table so smaller parties are less likely to consume flexible larger tables during the same dinner rush.
